sourceText: By compiling the 'Orthodox Confession of Faith' to systematize doctrine and by founding the Mohyla Academy to cultivate a new generation of clergy and scholars.
translatedText: Kwa kuandaa 'Ukiri wa Imani wa Orthodox' ili kuweka mafundisho kwa utaratibu na kwa kuanzisha Chuo cha Mohyla ili kukuza kizazi kipya cha makasisi na wasomi.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky
translatedText: Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky

sourceText: Lifelong depression, societal prejudice and internal conflict, a tragic marriage.
translatedText: Unyogovu wa maisha yote, ubaguzi wa kijamii na migogoro ya ndani, ndoa ya kutisha.

sourceText: Emotional sublimation through music, financial and emotional support from his patron Nadezhda von Meck.
translatedText: Usaidizi wa kihisia kupitia muziki, usaidizi wa kifedha na kihisia kutoka kwa mlinzi wake Nadezhda von Meck.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Witnessing the immense suffering caused by constant warfare and the moral corruption of the imperial court.
translatedText: Kushuhudia mateso makubwa yanayosababishwa na vita vya mara kwa mara na ufisadi wa kiadili wa mahakama ya kifalme.

sourceText: He channeled his disillusionment into his writing, creating a public history to praise the emperor's successes and a secret history to expose the regime's dark side.
translatedText: Alielekeza kukatishwa tamaa kwake katika uandishi wake, akitengeneza historia ya umma ya kusifia mafanikio ya mfalme huyo na historia ya siri ya kufichua upande wa giza wa utawala huo.

sourceText: 6th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Byzantine ya Karne ya 6

sourceText: After decades of war to save the empire from the Persians, I had to watch helplessly as the holy lands of Syria, Palestine, and Egypt fell to the new Arab armies.
translatedText: Baada ya miongo kadhaa ya vita ili kuokoa milki hiyo kutoka kwa Waajemi, ilinibidi kutazama bila msaada wakati ardhi takatifu za Syria, Palestina, na Misri zikiangukia kwa majeshi mapya ya Waarabu.

sourceText: I personally led my armies, employing bold strategies to achieve a decisive victory over Persia. I also reformed the imperial administration and military, establishing the Theme system that would be the backbone of the Empire for centuries.
translatedText: Mimi binafsi niliongoza majeshi yangu, nikitumia mbinu za ujasiri ili kupata ushindi mnono dhidi ya Uajemi. Pia nilirekebisha utawala wa kifalme na kijeshi, nikaanzisha mfumo wa Mandhari ambao ungekuwa uti wa mgongo wa Dola kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 7th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 7

sourceText: The frustration with contradictory and incomplete Buddhist texts in China, and the perilous, unsanctioned journey across deserts and mountains.
translatedText: Kuchanganyikiwa kwa maandishi ya Kibuddha yanayopingana na kutokamilika nchini China, na safari ya hatari, isiyo na kibali katika jangwa na milima.

sourceText: Successfully returned with over 650 Buddhist texts, founded the Yogacara school in China, and left a detailed record of his travels, influencing cultures for centuries.
translatedText: Alifanikiwa kurudi na maandishi zaidi ya 650 ya Kibuddha, akaanzisha shule ya Yogacara nchini China, na kuacha rekodi ya kina ya safari zake, na kuathiri tamaduni kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 7th Century Tang Dynasty
translatedText: Nasaba ya Tang ya Karne ya 7

sourceText: Hryhorii Skovoroda
translatedText: Hryhorii Skovoroda

sourceText: Rejection of worldly success, institutional constraints, and a life of constant wandering.
translatedText: Kukataliwa kwa mafanikio ya kidunia, vikwazo vya kitaasisi, na maisha ya kutangatanga.

sourceText: Found true happiness not in possessions or status, but through a philosophy of inner freedom and self-knowledge.
translatedText: Kupatikana furaha ya kweli si katika mali au hadhi, bali kupitia falsafa ya uhuru wa ndani na kujijua.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Gertrude Bell
translatedText: Gertrude Bell

sourceText: Navigating a male-dominated world of politics and exploration, and suffering personal heartbreaks, including the deaths of two men she loved.
translatedText: Kupitia ulimwengu unaotawaliwa na wanaume wa siasa na uvumbuzi, na kuteseka huzuni za kibinafsi, ikiwa ni pamoja na vifo vya wanaume wawili aliowapenda.

sourceText: She channeled her intellect and passion into her work, earning the respect of British officials and Arab leaders alike, and leaving a lasting political and cultural legacy.
translatedText: Alielekeza akili na mapenzi yake katika kazi yake, na kupata heshima ya maafisa wa Uingereza na viongozi wa Kiarabu sawa, na kuacha urithi wa kudumu wa kisiasa na kitamaduni.

sourceText: Late 19th and Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 na mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Constant threats of rebellion, betrayal from those closest to me, and the paranoia that consumed my later years.
translatedText: Vitisho vya mara kwa mara vya uasi, usaliti kutoka kwa wale walio karibu nami, na hali ya wasiwasi iliyochukua miaka yangu ya baadaye.

sourceText: Through decisive military victories and ruthless consolidation of power, I restored the Persian Empire. However, the trust I lost was never recovered.
translatedText: Kupitia ushindi madhubuti wa kijeshi na uimarishaji usio na huruma wa mamlaka, nilirejesha Milki ya Uajemi. Walakini, uaminifu niliopoteza haukupatikana tena.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Namık Kemal
translatedText: Jina la Kemal

sourceText: Witnessing the decline of the Ottoman Empire under absolute monarchy and facing constant persecution, including exile and imprisonment, for my political ideas.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kuporomoka kwa Milki ya Ottoman chini ya ufalme kamili na kukabiliwa na mateso ya mara kwa mara, ikiwa ni pamoja na uhamisho na kufungwa, kwa mawazo yangu ya kisiasa.

sourceText: By continuing to write prolifically—plays, novels, and newspaper articles—even from exile, spreading the ideals of constitutionalism and patriotism to a new generation.
translatedText: Kwa kuendelea kuandika kwa wingi-tamthilia, riwaya na makala za magazeti-hata kutoka uhamishoni, kueneza maadili ya utii wa katiba na uzalendo kwa kizazi kipya.

sourceText: 19th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Ottoman ya Karne ya 19

sourceText: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi
translatedText: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi

sourceText: Political turmoil and being held captive by the Assassins in their mountain fortresses.
translatedText: Msukosuko wa kisiasa na kuwa mateka na Wauaji katika ngome zao za milimani.

sourceText: Gaining the patronage of the Mongol ruler Hulagu Khan to establish the Maragheh observatory, a center of scientific learning.
translatedText: Kupata upendeleo wa mtawala wa Mongol Hulagu Khan kuanzisha uchunguzi wa Maragheh, kituo cha masomo ya kisayansi.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Constant rebellions, external threats to my empire's stability, and periods of profound mental anguish, which some say were a divine judgment.
translatedText: Uasi wa mara kwa mara, vitisho vya nje kwa uthabiti wa himaya yangu, na vipindi vya uchungu mwingi wa kiakili, ambao wengine wanasema ni hukumu ya kimungu.

sourceText: Through decisive military campaigns to crush dissent and by undertaking monumental construction projects to glorify the gods and demonstrate the power of my empire.
translatedText: Kupitia kampeni madhubuti za kijeshi za kukandamiza upinzani na kwa kufanya miradi mikubwa ya ujenzi ili kutukuza miungu na kuonyesha uwezo wa milki yangu.

sourceText: 7th-6th Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 7-6 KK

sourceText: Nizam al-Mulk
translatedText: Nizam al-Mulk

sourceText: Navigating the treacherous court politics, managing a vast and diverse empire, and confronting the rising threat of the Assassins (Hashshashin).
translatedText: Kupitia siasa za hila za mahakama, kusimamia himaya kubwa na tofauti, na kukabiliana na tishio linaloongezeka la Wauaji (Hashshashin).

sourceText: By creating a robust administrative bureaucracy, promoting Sunni orthodoxy through education, and authoring the 'Siyasatnama' (Book of Government) to guide future rulers.
translatedText: Kwa kuunda urasimu thabiti wa utawala, kukuza itikadi za Kisunni kupitia elimu, na kuandika 'Siyasatnama' (Kitabu cha Serikali) ili kuwaongoza watawala wa siku zijazo.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11

sourceText: I faced the immense challenge of legitimizing my rule after seizing the throne and had to quell widespread rebellions across the empire.
translatedText: Nilikabiliwa na changamoto kubwa ya kuhalalisha utawala wangu baada ya kutwaa kiti cha enzi na ilinibidi kuzima uasi ulioenea katika himaya yote.

sourceText: I asserted my divine right to rule through the Behistun Inscription, crushed the rebellions with military might, and then unified the empire through administrative genius, creating the satrapy system and the Royal Road.
translatedText: Nilidai haki yangu ya kimungu ya kutawala kupitia Maandishi ya Behistun, nikaponda waasi kwa nguvu za kijeshi, na kisha kuunganisha ufalme kupitia ujuzi wa utawala, kuunda mfumo wa satrapy na Barabara ya Kifalme.

sourceText: 6th-5th Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 6-5 KK

sourceText: Rashid Rida
translatedText: Rashid Rida

sourceText: Witnessing the decline of the Ottoman Empire and the intellectual stagnation of the Muslim world under the pressure of European colonialism.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kuporomoka kwa Dola ya Ottoman na kudumaa kwa kielimu kwa ulimwengu wa Kiislamu chini ya shinikizo la ukoloni wa Ulaya.

sourceText: By advocating for a return to the Quran and Sunnah (Salafism) and using his journal, Al-Manar, to spread reformist ideas across the Islamic world.
translatedText: Kwa kutetea kurejeshwa kwa Quran na Sunnah (Salafism) na kutumia jarida lake, Al-Manar, kueneza mawazo ya mageuzi katika ulimwengu wa Kiislamu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Foreign domination and internal fragmentation
translatedText: Utawala wa kigeni na mgawanyiko wa ndani

sourceText: Centralization of power and sweeping modernization
translatedText: Centralization ya nguvu na yanayojitokeza kisasa

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Persecuted as a heretic by the Zoroastrian priesthood and eventually imprisoned and executed under the Sasanian emperor Bahram I.
translatedText: Aliteswa kama mzushi na ukuhani wa Zoroasta na hatimaye kufungwa na kuuawa chini ya mfalme wa Sasania Bahram I.

sourceText: Despite his martyrdom, his disciples successfully spread his teachings across the ancient world, from the Roman Empire to China.
translatedText: Licha ya kuuawa kwake, wanafunzi wake walifanikiwa kueneza mafundisho yake katika ulimwengu wa kale, kutoka Milki ya Roma hadi Uchina.

sourceText: 3rd Century Sasanian Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Wasasania ya Karne ya 3

sourceText: The relentless opposition from the conservative Janissary corps and the constant threats to the empire's integrity from both internal rebellions and foreign powers.
translatedText: Upinzani usiokoma kutoka kwa kikosi cha kihafidhina cha Janissary na vitisho vya mara kwa mara kwa uadilifu wa dola kutoka kwa waasi wa ndani na nguvu za kigeni.

sourceText: By decisively dismantling the Janissaries in the 'Auspicious Incident' and establishing a new, modern army, I was able to push through my agenda of centralized reform.
translatedText: Kwa kuwavunjilia mbali Janissaries katika 'Tukio Adhimu' na kuanzisha jeshi jipya la kisasa, niliweza kusukuma ajenda yangu ya mageuzi ya serikali kuu.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Ascending to the throne at a young age amidst court intrigue and external threats, and the immense challenge of breaching the 'impenetrable' walls of Constantinople.
translatedText: Kupanda kwenye kiti cha enzi katika umri mdogo kati ya fitina za mahakama na vitisho vya nje, na changamoto kubwa ya kuvunja kuta 'zisizopenyeka' za Konstantinople.

sourceText: Through meticulous planning, the use of innovative siege technology like massive cannons, and unwavering resolve, I overcame all opposition to achieve my destiny.
translatedText: Kupitia kupanga kwa uangalifu, matumizi ya teknolojia ya ubunifu ya kuzingirwa kama mizinga mikubwa, na azimio lisiloyumbayumba, nilishinda upinzani wote ili kufikia hatima yangu.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 15

sourceText: The collapse of the Ottoman Empire, foreign occupation, and the deep-rooted resistance to the modernization my country desperately needed.
translatedText: Kuporomoka kwa Milki ya Ottoman, uvamizi wa kigeni, na upinzani wa kina wa uboreshaji wa kisasa ambao nchi yangu ilihitaji sana.

sourceText: By leading the Turkish War of Independence to victory, abolishing the Sultanate, and establishing a republic. I then implemented a series of radical reforms to create a modern, secular nation-state.
translatedText: Kwa kuongoza Vita vya Uhuru vya Uturuki kwa ushindi, kukomesha Usultani, na kuanzisha jamhuri. Kisha nikatekeleza msururu wa mageuzi makubwa ili kuunda taifa la kisasa, lisilo na dini.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Navigating the decline of the Ottoman Empire while fending off the imperial ambitions of European powers like Britain and France.
translatedText: Kupitia kudorora kwa Milki ya Ottoman huku kikilinda matamanio ya kifalme ya madola ya Ulaya kama Uingereza na Ufaransa.

sourceText: By building a formidable, European-style army and navy, centralizing the state, and monopolizing trade, I created a powerful, independent base in Egypt.
translatedText: Kwa kuunda jeshi la kutisha, la mtindo wa Uropa na jeshi la wanamaji, kuweka serikali kuu, na kuhodhi biashara, niliunda msingi wenye nguvu, huru nchini Misri.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Muhammad Abduh
translatedText: Muhammad Abduh

sourceText: The stagnation of Islamic societies, the dogmatism of traditionalist scholars, and the overwhelming pressure of European colonialism.
translatedText: Kudorora kwa jamii za Kiislamu, imani ya sharti ya wanazuoni wa jadi, na shinikizo kubwa la ukoloni wa Ulaya.

sourceText: Championing the use of reason (ijtihad) to reinterpret Islamic texts for contemporary times and advocating for widespread educational reform to revitalize the Muslim world.
translatedText: Kupigania matumizi ya akili (ijtihad) kutafsiri upya maandishi ya Kiislamu kwa zama za kisasa na kutetea mageuzi makubwa ya elimu ili kuhuisha ulimwengu wa Kiislamu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Mimar Sinan
translatedText: Mimar Sinan

sourceText: The sorrow of being taken from my family at a young age under the Devşirme system to serve as a soldier in a foreign land.
translatedText: Huzuni ya kuondolewa kutoka kwa familia yangu katika umri mdogo chini ya mfumo wa Devşirme kutumikia kama askari katika nchi ya kigeni.

sourceText: By honing my engineering and architectural skills during military service, I caught the Sultan's attention and was appointed Chief Imperial Architect, allowing me to fully realize my talents.
translatedText: Kwa kuboresha ustadi wangu wa uhandisi na usanifu wakati wa utumishi wa kijeshi, nilivutia uangalifu wa Sultani na nikawekwa rasmi kuwa Msanifu Mkuu wa Imperial, na kuniwezesha kutambua kikamili vipaji vyangu.

sourceText: 16th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Ottoman ya Karne ya 16

sourceText: Badi' al-Zaman al-Hamadani
translatedText: Badi' al-Zaman al-Hamadani

sourceText: Constant travel and rivalry with other scholars and poets to secure patronage.
translatedText: Kusafiri mara kwa mara na kushindana na wasomi wengine na washairi ili kupata udhamini.

sourceText: His unparalleled linguistic skill and the invention of the Maqama secured his fame and influence for centuries.
translatedText: Ustadi wake wa lugha usio na kifani na uvumbuzi wa Maqama ulipata umaarufu na ushawishi wake kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 10th-11th Century / Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Karne ya 10-11 / Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Kiislamu

sourceText: Bediüzzaman Said Nursi
translatedText: Bediuzzaman Said Nursi

sourceText: Decades of exile, imprisonment, and persecution under the secular Turkish Republic for his religious teachings.
translatedText: Miongo kadhaa ya uhamisho, kifungo, na mateso chini ya Jamhuri ya Kituruki isiyo ya kidini kwa mafundisho yake ya kidini.

sourceText: He transformed his suffering into a spiritual victory by writing the 6,000-page Risale-i Nur, which created a powerful spiritual revival.
translatedText: Alibadilisha mateso yake kuwa ushindi wa kiroho kwa kuandika Risale-i Nur ya kurasa 6,000, ambayo iliunda uamsho wenye nguvu wa kiroho.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: A brutal childhood, captured on the Kipchak Steppe and sold into slavery.
translatedText: Utoto wa kikatili, ulitekwa kwenye nyika ya Kipchak na kuuzwa utumwani.

sourceText: Rising through the Mamluk ranks through sheer military genius and ambition, ultimately seizing the sultanate and defending the Islamic world.
translatedText: Kupanda kwa safu ya Wamamluk kupitia ujuzi kamili wa kijeshi na tamaa, hatimaye kunyakua usultani na kutetea ulimwengu wa Kiislamu.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: Saadi Shirazi
translatedText: Nimepata Shiraz

sourceText: The Mongol invasion of Persia, which forced him into decades of wandering across the known world.
translatedText: Uvamizi wa Mongol wa Uajemi, ambao ulimlazimu katika miongo kadhaa ya kutangatanga katika ulimwengu unaojulikana.

sourceText: He transformed his experiences as a traveler and observer of humanity into timeless works of poetry and prose.
translatedText: Alibadilisha uzoefu wake kama msafiri na mwangalizi wa ubinadamu kuwa kazi zisizo na wakati za mashairi na nathari.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 13

sourceText: My path to power was fraught with peril, from my obscure birth to the treachery of King Ur-Zababa of Kish, whom I served.
translatedText: Njia yangu ya kwenda madarakani ilikuwa imejaa hatari, kutoka kwa kuzaliwa kwangu kusikojulikana hadi kwa usaliti wa Mfalme Ur-Zababa wa Kishi, ambaye nilimtumikia.

sourceText: Through military prowess and divine favor, I overthrew my rivals, united the disparate city-states of Sumer, and forged an empire that stretched across Mesopotamia.
translatedText: Kupitia uhodari wa kijeshi na upendeleo wa kimungu, niliwapindua wapinzani wangu, nikaunganisha majimbo yenye tofauti ya miji ya Sumer, na kuunda milki iliyoenea kote Mesopotamia.

sourceText: 24th Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 24 KK

sourceText: Constant military pressure from the Roman Empire on my western frontiers and managing the diverse religious landscape within my vast domain.
translatedText: Shinikizo la mara kwa mara la kijeshi kutoka kwa Milki ya Kirumi kwenye mipaka yangu ya magharibi na kusimamia mazingira tofauti ya kidini ndani ya kikoa changu kikubwa.

sourceText: Achieving a decisive victory at the Battle of Edessa, capturing an emperor, and implementing a policy of religious tolerance that fostered internal stability and growth.
translatedText: Kufikia ushindi madhubuti kwenye Vita vya Edessa, kumkamata mfalme, na kutekeleza sera ya uvumilivu wa kidini ambayo ilikuza utulivu na ukuaji wa ndani.

sourceText: 3rd Century Sasanian Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Wasasania ya Karne ya 3

sourceText: My desperate attempts at reform were met with fierce resistance from conservative forces, particularly the Janissaries and the Ulama, leading to their ultimate failure.
translatedText: Majaribio yangu ya kukatisha tamaa ya kuleta mageuzi yalikabiliwa na upinzani mkali kutoka kwa vikosi vya wahafidhina, hasa Janissaries na Ulamaa, na kusababisha kushindwa kwao kabisa.

sourceText: Though my reign ended in tragedy and my reforms were undone, my vision planted the seeds for the later Tanzimat reforms, which became a crucial foundation for Ottoman modernization.
translatedText: Ingawa utawala wangu uliishia kwa msiba na mageuzi yangu yakabatilishwa, maono yangu yalipanda mbegu kwa ajili ya mageuzi ya baadaye ya Tanzimat, ambayo yalikuja kuwa msingi muhimu wa uboreshaji wa Ottoman.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: I ascended the throne in a time of chaos, with my kingdom torn apart by internal strife among the Qizilbash factions and external threats from the Ottomans and Uzbeks.
translatedText: Nilipanda kiti cha enzi katika wakati wa machafuko, huku ufalme wangu ukiwa umesambaratishwa na ugomvi wa ndani kati ya vikundi vya Qizilbash na vitisho vya nje kutoka kwa Waothmani na Uzbekistan.

sourceText: I ruthlessly centralized power, creating a standing army of 'ghulams' loyal only to me, breaking the power of the tribal chieftains. Through military genius and shrewd diplomacy, I reclaimed lost territories and ushered in an era of unprecedented prosperity.
translatedText: Kwa ukatili niliweka mamlaka kuu, nikiunda jeshi lililosimama la 'ghulams' watiifu kwangu tu, na kuvunja mamlaka ya wakuu wa makabila. Kupitia ujuzi wa kijeshi na diplomasia ya busara, nilirudisha maeneo yaliyopotea na kuanzisha enzi ya ufanisi usio na kifani.

sourceText: Late 16th - Early 17th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 16 - Mapema karne ya 17

sourceText: Ashurbanipal
translatedText: Ashurbanipal

sourceText: The betrayal of my own brother, Shamash-shum-ukin, which led to a devastating civil war, and the constant need to suppress rebellions across my vast empire.
translatedText: Usaliti wa kaka yangu mwenyewe, Shamash-shum-ukin, ambao ulisababisha vita vya wenyewe kwa wenyewe vyenye uharibifu, na hitaji la mara kwa mara la kukandamiza uasi katika himaya yangu kubwa.

sourceText: Through decisive military victories and a ruthless assertion of power, I crushed the rebellion and restored order, channeling my energy into creating a legacy of culture and knowledge.
translatedText: Kupitia ushindi madhubuti wa kijeshi na uthibitisho usio na huruma wa mamlaka, nilikandamiza uasi na kurejesha utulivu, nikielekeza nguvu zangu katika kuunda urithi wa utamaduni na ujuzi.

sourceText: 7th Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 7 KK

sourceText: Ahmed Shawqi
translatedText: Ahmed Shawqi

sourceText: Forced into exile in Spain for his nationalist views against British rule.
translatedText: Alilazimishwa kwenda uhamishoni Uhispania kwa maoni yake ya utaifa dhidi ya utawala wa Waingereza.

sourceText: He channeled his longing for home into powerful poetry, celebrating Arab history and identity, which solidified his status as a national icon upon his return.
translatedText: Alielekeza hamu yake ya kutaka nyumbani katika ushairi wenye nguvu, akisherehekea historia ya Waarabu na utambulisho wake, ambao uliimarisha hadhi yake kama sanamu ya kitaifa aliporudi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Ahmed Cevdet Pasha
translatedText: Ahmed Cevdet Pasha

sourceText: Witnessing the decline of the empire while navigating the intense conflict between tradition and modernization.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kudorora kwa himaya huku tukipitia mzozo mkali kati ya mila na usasa.

sourceText: Attempting to strengthen the empire's legal and intellectual foundations through comprehensive legal reform and historical scholarship.
translatedText: Kujaribu kuimarisha misingi ya kisheria na kiakili ya himaya kupitia mageuzi ya kina ya kisheria na usomi wa kihistoria.

sourceText: 19th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Ottoman ya Karne ya 19

sourceText: Rhazes (Al-Razi)
translatedText: Rhazes (Al-Razi)

sourceText: I faced criticism from religious dogmatists who opposed my rationalist and philosophical views, and I grappled with the limits of medical knowledge in my time.
translatedText: Nilikosolewa na wafuasi wa imani za kidini ambao walipinga maoni yangu ya kiakili na ya kifalsafa, na nilipambana na mipaka ya ujuzi wa kitiba katika wakati wangu.

sourceText: By championing empirical evidence, conducting meticulous experiments, and documenting my findings, I established new medical truths and defended the pursuit of knowledge through reason.
translatedText: Kwa kutetea uthibitisho wa kimatibabu, kufanya majaribio ya kina, na kuandika mambo niliyogundua, nilianzisha kweli mpya za kitiba na kutetea kufuatia ujuzi kupitia akili.

sourceText: Islamic Golden Age (9th-10th Century)
translatedText: Umri wa Dhahabu wa Kiislamu (karne ya 9-10)

sourceText: A devastating civil war against my half-brother, al-Amin, for the caliphate, which fractured the empire.
translatedText: Vita mbaya ya wenyewe kwa wenyewe dhidi ya kaka yangu wa kambo, al-Amin, kwa ajili ya ukhalifa, ambayo ilivunja himaya.

sourceText: By securing military victory and promoting intellectual and cultural unity through the House of Wisdom, I consolidated power and ushered in an unparalleled era of scientific advancement.
translatedText: Kwa kupata ushindi wa kijeshi na kukuza umoja wa kiakili na kitamaduni kupitia Nyumba ya Hekima, niliunganisha mamlaka na kuanzisha enzi isiyo na kifani ya maendeleo ya kisayansi.

sourceText: Early 9th Century
translatedText: Mapema karne ya 9

sourceText: The loss of many of my extensive writings, including the vast majority of my 30-volume universal history.
translatedText: Kupotea kwa maandishi yangu mengi, kutia ndani sehemu kubwa ya historia yangu ya jumla ya juzuu 30.

sourceText: My surviving works, especially 'The Meadows of Gold', established a new, encyclopedic approach to history and geography that influenced scholars for centuries.
translatedText: Kazi zangu zilizosalia, haswa 'The Meadows of Gold', zilianzisha mkabala mpya wa encyclopedic wa historia na jiografia ambao uliathiri wasomi kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 10th Century, Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Karne ya 10, Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Kiislamu

sourceText: Working with the limited observational tools of my time and challenging the long-held, almost dogmatic, conclusions of Ptolemy.
translatedText: Kufanya kazi na zana chache za uchunguzi za wakati wangu na kupinga mahitimisho ya muda mrefu, karibu ya kidogma, ya Ptolemy.

sourceText: Through decades of persistent, meticulous observation and rigorous mathematical analysis, I compiled my own data to refine and correct ancient theories.
translatedText: Kupitia miongo kadhaa ya uchunguzi unaoendelea, wa kina na uchambuzi wa kina wa hisabati, nilikusanya data yangu ili kuboresha na kusahihisha nadharia za zamani.

sourceText: 9th-10th Century Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Kiislamu ya Karne ya 9-10

sourceText: Social prejudice and mockery due to my humble origins and perceived ugliness.
translatedText: Ubaguzi wa kijamii na dhihaka kwa sababu ya asili yangu duni na ubaya unaoonekana.

sourceText: I overcame this through sheer intellectual brilliance, wit, and prolific writing, earning the patronage of caliphs and becoming a leading intellectual.
translatedText: Nililishinda hili kwa ustadi mkubwa wa kiakili, akili, na uandishi mwingi, nikipata ufadhili wa makhalifa na kuwa msomi mkuu.

sourceText: 9th Century Abbasid Caliphate
translatedText: Ukhalifa wa Bani Abbas wa Karne ya 9

sourceText: Faced political persecution and the jealousy of rivals, which led to the confiscation of his personal library and a temporary fall from favor.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na mateso ya kisiasa na wivu wa wapinzani, ambayo ilisababisha kunyang'anywa maktaba yake ya kibinafsi na kuanguka kwa upendeleo kwa muda.

sourceText: Through the intervention of a powerful friend and his unwavering commitment to knowledge, he eventually had his library returned and his status restored, allowing him to continue his work.
translatedText: Kupitia uingiliaji kati wa rafiki mwenye nguvu na kujitolea kwake bila kuyumbayumba kwa maarifa, hatimaye maktaba yake ikarejeshwa na hali yake kurejeshwa, na kumruhusu kuendelea na kazi yake.

sourceText: 9th Century Abbasid Caliphate
translatedText: Ukhalifa wa Bani Abbas wa Karne ya 9

sourceText: Faced intense opposition from established legal schools when he tried to found his own (the Jariri school), which ultimately failed. He was even placed under house arrest in Baghdad in his final years.
translatedText: Alikabiliwa na upinzani mkali kutoka kwa shule za kisheria zilizoanzishwa alipojaribu kutafuta shule yake (shule ya Jariri), ambayo hatimaye ilifeli. Hata aliwekwa chini ya kizuizi cha nyumbani huko Baghdad katika miaka yake ya mwisho.

sourceText: Despite these jurisprudential and political setbacks, he devoted himself fully to his scholarship, producing his monumental works of history and commentary that have endured for centuries.
translatedText: Licha ya vikwazo hivi vya kifiqhi na kisiasa, alijitolea kikamilifu katika usomi wake, akitoa kazi zake kuu za historia na maelezo ambayo yamedumu kwa karne nyingi.

sourceText: 9th-10th Century, Islamic Golden Age
translatedText: Karne ya 9-10, Enzi ya Dhahabu ya Kiislamu

sourceText: Constant threats from rival powers, internal strife, and the immense burden of leading a vast and diverse empire.
translatedText: Vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa mamlaka zinazoshindana, ugomvi wa ndani, na mzigo mkubwa wa kuongoza himaya kubwa na tofauti.

sourceText: Through military prowess, strategic alliances, and a firm vision for a unified Sunni state, I secured the empire's borders and legacy.
translatedText: Kupitia uhodari wa kijeshi, ushirikiano wa kimkakati, na maono madhubuti ya kuwa na taifa moja la Sunni, nililinda mipaka na urithi wa milki hiyo.

sourceText: 11th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 11

sourceText: Evliya Çelebi
translatedText: Evliya Çelebi

sourceText: Constant threats to my life from wars, plagues, political turmoil, and the sheer dangers of travel in the 17th century.
translatedText: Vitisho vya mara kwa mara kwa maisha yangu kutokana na vita, tauni, misukosuko ya kisiasa, na hatari kubwa za kusafiri katika karne ya 17.

sourceText: My insatiable curiosity, a profound sense of duty to document the world, and the patronage of powerful sultans and pashas allowed me to continue my forty-year journey.
translatedText: Udadisi wangu usiotosheka, hisia kubwa ya wajibu wa kuandika ulimwengu, na ulezi wa masultani wenye nguvu na wapasha uliniruhusu kuendelea na safari yangu ya miaka arobaini.

sourceText: 17th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Ottoman ya Karne ya 17

sourceText: Ibrahim Muteferrika
translatedText: Ibrahim Muteferrika

sourceText: Religious and social resistance to printing, coupled with technical challenges.
translatedText: Upinzani wa kidini na kijamii dhidi ya uchapishaji, pamoja na changamoto za kiufundi.

sourceText: Gained official permission through the Sultan's patronage and persistent advocacy, successfully establishing his press.
translatedText: Alipata kibali rasmi kupitia ulezi wa Sultani na utetezi unaoendelea, na kufanikiwa kuanzisha vyombo vyake vya habari.

sourceText: 18th Century Ottoman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Ottoman ya Karne ya 18

sourceText: The constant intervention of European powers, which ultimately forced me to relinquish my conquest of Syria and thwarted my father's grand ambitions for a regional empire.
translatedText: Kuingilia mara kwa mara kwa mataifa makubwa ya Ulaya, ambayo hatimaye yalinilazimisha kuacha ushindi wangu wa Siria na kuzuia matarajio makuu ya baba yangu ya milki ya kikanda.

sourceText: Through decisive military victories and effective administration in the lands I conquered, I demonstrated Egypt's power and laid the groundwork for its modern statehood, even if my rule was brief.
translatedText: Kupitia ushindi madhubuti wa kijeshi na utawala bora katika nchi nilizoziteka, nilionyesha uwezo wa Misri na kuweka msingi wa serikali yake ya kisasa, hata kama utawala wangu ulikuwa mfupi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Misunderstanding and condemnation from orthodox scholars who found my teachings controversial.
translatedText: Kutokuelewana na kulaaniwa kutoka kwa wasomi wa Orthodox ambao walipata mafundisho yangu kuwa na utata.
